title: Council thinker return (Phase 2)
description: Validates one council thinker's structured return inside council-parallel (SPEC-workflow-phases-2-5.md § 2a, § 2c). Each thinker (model: opus, $0 tools — thinker.md disallows web tools) writes a gap-first analysis; the barrier validates every return against this schema, re-dispatches once on failure, and records an unrecoverable failure in the orchestrator's missing[] (never silently dropped). statutory_tags[] $refs the shared statutory-evidence schema so a thinker's statutory claims carry the same {url, quote} evidence contract the gates verify against (F6). additionalProperties:false so a thinker cannot smuggle an unschema'd field; FLAG-only posture — a thinker reports gaps and positions, never replacement text.

perspective_name.description: The perspective this thinker argued from (e.g. 'Compensation Strategist', 'Industry Outsider: pharma').

core_position.description: The thinker's central position on the topic.

knowledge_gaps.description: What this perspective flags as unknown or unverified — the gap-first discipline. Empty array = no gaps surfaced (report [], never omit).

recommendations.description: Concrete recommendations from this perspective. Empty array allowed.

statutory_tags.description: Optional. Statutory/market claims this thinker made, each with its {url, quote} evidence (shared statutory-evidence schema). An item with an empty quote is asserted-but-not-confirmed (invariant #5) — never read it as confirmation.

confidence_tag_counts.description: Optional. Per-confidence-tag counts across this thinker's claims (comp-suite tag vocabulary, e.g. CONSENSUS / SINGLE_SOURCE / REPORTED / UNVERIFIED / UNRESEARCHED). Keys are tag names; values are non-negative counts. Advisory orientation, not a quality score.
